meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategies to Solve Founder Burnout & Project Stalls
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS. Learn how strategic pricing solves project completion issues. Master your MVP with Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: The inability to finish projects often stems from feature creep disguised as 'perfecting' the product. By implementing a strict, value-based pricing model early, you force yourself to prioritize only the core features users are willing to pay for, effectively killing the scope creep that keeps your project in perpetual development.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: the prototype is gorgeous, the stack is bleeding-edge, but the 'Launch' button remains a distant, terrifying mirage. You aren't suffering from a lack of technical skill; you are suffering from a lack of market constraints. When you build without a price tag, you build for an infinite scope, and infinite scope is the graveyard of SaaS dreams.

True velocity doesn't come from working faster; it comes from working on the right things. By anchoring your development in a specific, paid pricing tier, you transform your project from a 'passion hobby' into a commercial engine. It’s time to stop polishing pixels and start shipping value that customers actually want to finance.

steps.title: Define the Value Metric
steps.description: Identify the one metric that correlates with user success (e.g., messages sent, files processed). This narrows your development focus to the core revenue driver.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and propose three value-based pricing metrics that minimize feature complexity while maximizing perceived customer value.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing_model.md file outlining the chosen value metric and its impact on development scope.'

steps.title: Architect for the MVP Tier
steps.description: Strip away all non-essential features that don't support your primary pricing tier. If it isn't in the MVP, it doesn't exist.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current feature list and categorize them into 'Launch' vs 'Post-Revenue'. Remove anything that doesn't support the core subscription tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the project roadmap to exclude all features not essential for the initial paid MVP.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Integration
steps.description: Early integration forces you to confront the reality of payments, which is the ultimate cure for project procrastination.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean Stripe Checkout implementation for a single-tier subscription model using TypeScript.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Install stripe-node and scaffold the subscription checkout flow in the main application route.'

steps.title: Automate Value Tracking
steps.description: Build a lightweight dashboard that tracks your value metric so you can prove worth to your first paying users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a backend service that tracks user usage against the defined pricing metric and triggers a paywall if limits are exceeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the usage-tracking middleware to monitor subscription limits for authenticated users.'

steps.title: Deploy the 'Kill-Switch' MVP
steps.description: Ship your product to a staging environment with live pricing enabled. The fear of charging money is the best motivator to finish.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Prepare a deployment script that sets up a production-ready environment with environment variable validation for Stripe keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Validate deployment readiness and generate a deployment summary for the production environment.'

pro_tips: Always charge for your MVP, even if it's a nominal fee; free users don't provide the feedback required to finish a product.
Use 'Time-to-First-Dollar' as your primary KPI, not lines of code or feature count.
If you find yourself stuck on a feature for more than 48 hours, it's likely not essential to your pricing model—cut it.
Leverage Claude Code to document your 'No-List'—the things you explicitly agree not to build until you reach your first 10 paying customers.
Focus on a single user persona; trying to price for multiple segments early will bloat your codebase and kill your momentum.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, perform a 'smoke test' by sending your link to 5 potential users. Do not add another feature until you have successfully processed a real transaction.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the strategy to stop building in circles and start building a business. Don't let another project stall because of scope creep. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
